title: PERSONNEL FILE: KIRIKO
subtitle: Field Intelligence Operative | Formerly SCP-6714
author: Human Resources, Intelligence Agency
summary: Former contained anomaly turned Foundation intelligence asset. Full record includes operational history and biography.
body:
## TITLE I. Operational Information

**Serial #:** 847-206-913

**Clearance:** Level 3 (Secret)

**Classification:** Anomalous Personnel, Field Intelligence Operative

**Secondary Clearance:** Containment-Assist Authorization (Conditional, see Title VI)

**SCiPnet Handle:** kiriko.ia06

**Primary Department:** Intelligence Agency

**Rank:** Field Intelligence Operative, Grade II

**Sub-Groups:** None

**Sub-Group Rank:** N/A

**Assigned Task Force(s):** None (standing). Holds Honorary Auxiliary status with MTF Epsilon-11 "Nine-Tailed Fox" for the sole purpose of the containment-assist clause described in Title VI. This status carries no departmental authority and does not constitute reassignment.

**Specialization:** Long-duration deep-cover infiltration and disguise operations, informed by approximately one thousand years of continuous, lived exposure to shifting human societies, languages, and customs, an asset the Intelligence Agency has been unable to replicate through any other means.

**Previous Ranks:** Contained Anomalous Asset (SCP-APD-006, 2022 to 2026; redesignated SCP-6714, 2026)

**Supervisors:** Handler of record withheld at operative's own request; case oversight retained by Intelligence Agency Directorate.

**Outlook:** Exceptional. Kiriko's placement is considered one of the Intelligence Agency's most valuable acquisitions of the decade, though the Agency notes internally that this value was purchased at a cost it did not pay.

---

## TITLE II. Demographic Information

**Full Name:** Kiriko (surname unrecorded; see Biography)

**Known Alias(es):** SCP-APD-006, SCP-6714, "Fox-6" (operational callsign, MTF Epsilon-11 honorary designation)

**Date of Birth:** Unrecorded. Foundation estimate places Kiriko's emergence at approximately 1000 to 1100 CE, based on standard kitsune tail-development timelines and independent corroboration from period-consistent knowledge displayed during early debriefing.

**Place of Birth:** Honshu, Japan (specific region unrecorded)

**Race:** Kitsune (Anomalous)

**Gender:** Female-presenting. Kitsune folklore and Kiriko's own testimony both indicate the species does not hold fixed human categories of sex or gender to be especially meaningful.

**Nationality:** None. Kiriko predates the modern Japanese state by several centuries and has never held or sought citizenship anywhere. Formally classified as a Foundation Ward.

**Ethnicity:** N/A (Anomalous Species)

**Home Site:** Site-19

**Place of Residence:** Site-19, Staff Wing C, Unit 14

**Major Scars:** Extensive scarring along the left flank and shoulder, consistent with claw injuries, sustained during engagement with SCP-953. A faded, roughly circular scar on the inside of the left forearm, of anomalous origin, believed related to prior captivity.

**Tattoo/Markings:** A small alphanumeric brand on the back of the neck, partially obscured by hair, matching inventory markings later associated with the trafficking operation known internally as Verdigris Exotics. Kiriko has declined all offers of cosmetic removal.

**Eyewear:** None required. Kiriko's natural low-light vision exceeds standard-issue night vision equipment.

**Extra:** Fur and hair present in an uneven silver-and-green pattern, the green concentrated at the roots and tail-tips. This is not natural kitsune coloration. It is the permanent result of anomalous forced pigmentation and has not reverted despite years in both prior APD and current Foundation custody. Kiriko has been offered cosmetic correction on four occasions and has declined each time.

---

## Appearance Description

**Physical Description:** Kiriko presents as a woman in early adulthood by human visual standards, though this bears no relation to her true age. She stands approximately 5'6", with a slight, wiry build shaped more by centuries of practiced movement than by any conventional training regimen. Nine tails, silver at the base and darkening to a mottled, unnatural green toward the tips, are typically kept bound and concealed beneath clothing or a long coat during field operations, released only at will. Fox ears replace human ears entirely. Kiriko relies on a minor anomalous property allowing her to render both imperceptible to unfocused observation without physically altering them, rather than any illusion of a fully human appearance. Her eyes are a pale amber-gold, with vertically slit pupils in low light.

**Attire Description:** Field attire consists of a modified E-11 tactical vest, tailored to accommodate her tails, worn over dark civilian-cut clothing suited to blending into an urban environment on short notice. The vest, along with the sidearm and rifle described in Title VI, previously belonged to Lieutenant Marcus Kade. Kiriko has had none of it reissued in her own name. Foundation Quartermaster records still list all three items under Kade's service number, at Kiriko's specific and repeated request.

---

## TITLE III. Medical & Psychological Record

**Sex:** Female (see Gender, Title II)

**Hair Color:** Silver, with green discoloration (anomalous, non-reverting)

**Height:** 5'6" (168 cm)

**Eye Color:** Amber-gold

**Weight:** 128 lbs (58 kg)

**Ethnicity:** N/A

**Blood Type:** Not applicable in the conventional sense. Kiriko's physiology supports standard transfusion medicine using type O-negative in emergencies, per Site-19 Medical.

**Prescriptions:** None

**Allergies:** None recorded

**Conditions:** None formally recorded. Site-19 Medical flags recurring sleep disturbance in the months following the events described in the Biography; this has not been formally diagnosed at Kiriko's request and is monitored informally by her handler instead.

**Augments:** None

**Last Updated:** 2027-03-15

**Abstract:** Kiriko presents as reserved, watchful, and unfailingly polite to a degree several staff have described as "performed," in the sense of a habit maintained long after its original purpose stopped mattering. She warms slowly and, once she does, is fiercely loyal, to a fault her handlers have flagged more than once. She does not discuss her captivity under Verdigris Exotics unless directly and privately asked, and has never discussed it in detail with more than one person at Site-19.

**Virtues:** Loyalty, patience, precision, an old-fashioned sense of debt and obligation that Foundation psychological staff have both praised and worried over in the same report.

**Vices:** A tendency to take on risk that is not hers to take, particularly where it might spare someone else from taking it instead. Difficulty accepting help.

**Likes:** Quiet rooms. Green tea, an old habit predating the Foundation by centuries. The sound of a facility at night, after most personnel have gone to bed.

**Dislikes:** Being described as "recovered" or "healed." Cages of any kind, including ordinary elevators, though she rides them without comment. Being asked to justify her own value.

**Psychiatric Conditions:** None formally diagnosed. Staff psychologist notes indicate symptoms consistent with prolonged captivity trauma and complicated grief, both declined for formal diagnosis at the subject's request, a request Site-19's Ethics Committee has elected to honor given her legal status as personnel rather than a contained subject.

**Psychiatrist's Orders:** Do not bring up Lieutenant Kade by name in a debrief setting; allow Kiriko to raise the subject herself. Do not use the word "rescued." Do not schedule field assignments during the week of November 3rd without her explicit sign-off.

---

## TITLE IV. Legal & Ethical Information

**Injuries in Service:** Multiple lacerations to the left flank and shoulder sustained in direct combat with SCP-953 on 2026-11-03, requiring immediate surgical intervention. Full recovery was achieved within three weeks, attributed in part to standard kitsune healing rates, which Site-19 Medical notes are elevated but explicitly not instantaneous or anomalous in the manner Foundation policy would otherwise restrict.

**Security Encounters:** One unauthorized egress from containment during the Site-19 cascade event of 2026-11-03, immediately followed by voluntary cooperation with MTF Epsilon-11 field operations. No disciplinary action was pursued; see Biography.

**Ethical/Legal Violations:** None. The Ethics Committee's sole formal action regarding Kiriko was retroactive: a unanimous vote on 2026-12-11 to classify her prior captivity, rather than any of her own conduct, as the matter warranting review.

**Captain's Orders:** Standing order from MTF Epsilon-11 command, dated 2026-11-04: any member of Epsilon-11 present at Site-19 is to treat Kiriko's judgment on containment matters as equivalent to a field agent's, pending and now superseding formal reclassification. Order has not been rescinded.

---

## TITLE V. Biographical Information

**Relatives:** None known. Kiriko has stated, on the one occasion she discussed it, that any kitsune she once called kin predate Foundation record-keeping entirely and are presumed either dispersed or dead.

**Criminal Record:** None, in any jurisdiction, Foundation or civil.

**Relations:** Lieutenant Marcus Kade, MTF Epsilon-11 (deceased, 2026-11-03; see Biography). Captain Elena Osei, MTF Epsilon-11 (current primary point of contact). Dr. Naomi Farrow, Intelligence Agency (handler of record, named here despite Title I's withholding, at Kiriko's own preference for informal contexts).

**Education:** None in any formal institutional sense. Extensive self-directed knowledge of history, multiple human languages (Kiriko is conversationally fluent in no fewer than eleven, by her own count, and reads several dead ones), and applied folklore, all acquired through lived experience rather than instruction.

**Experience:** Approximately one thousand years of direct, continuous observation of human society, a qualification the Intelligence Agency has noted, without irony, no current staff member can compete with.

**Former Job:** Contained anomalous asset, APD-14 (2022 to 2026); contained anomalous asset, Site-19 (2026).

### ADDENDUM.CODENAME/II: Biography

« BEGIN TRANSCRIPT »

Kiriko does not remember a childhood in any sense a human researcher could file under that heading. What she has offered, across several long debriefs conducted more like conversations than interrogations, is this: she remembers Honshu before it had that name. She remembers shrines that no longer stand, and the particular quality of silence in a pine forest before dawn, and being warned, by kitsune older than her who are themselves long gone, that a fox with nine tails should never let itself be seen twice by the same human. She kept that rule for the better part of a millennium. It did not save her.

The organization that found her called itself, on paper, Verdigris Exotics, an unremarkable import-export front operating out of a series of warehouses that moved every few years to stay ahead of anyone looking too closely. Its actual business was the acquisition, alteration, and sale of anomalous creatures to private collectors willing to pay extraordinary sums for something no one else in the world could own. A nine-tailed kitsune, silver-coated and demonstrably ancient, was already a once-in-a-generation acquisition by the standards of that market. Verdigris wanted more than that. Internal documents recovered during the Delta-20 raid indicate the organization had begun a program of forced anomalous pigmentation, altering captured kitsune into colors folklore had never recorded, on the theory that a buyer would pay more for something that looked like nothing that had ever existed before. Kiriko was mid-treatment, silver bleeding into an unnatural green from the roots outward, when APD | Delta-20 breached the facility.

She has described the years of her captivity in detail exactly once, to exactly one person, and it is not reproduced here out of respect for that limitation. What is on record is simpler: Delta-20 cleared the facility room by room, and when a cell door built for something considerably less dangerous than a nine-tailed kitsune was found still welded shut, standard procedure called for it to be flagged for later disposal rather than opened blind. A member of the recovery team, against standard procedure, opened it anyway. Kiriko did not attack. She asked, in Japanese first and then in accented but functional English, whether the warehouse was on fire, because she could smell smoke and wanted to know if she should be afraid of that too, on top of everything else. It was not on fire. Delta-20 had simply started their breach with incendiary charges on the perimeter fence.

APD-14 catalogued her as SCP-APD-006 in the spring of 2022. Her four subsequent years in APD custody were, by her own account, the first genuinely stable period of her existence in longer than she could reliably measure. She was not free, and she has said she understood why she could not be, even at the time. But she was fed, spoken to as a person rather than an asset, and given, eventually, access to books, which she consumed at a rate that alarmed APD's research staff before it began to please them. When APD's license was suspended in June of 2026 and its remaining contained assets began transferring to direct Foundation custody, Kiriko's file moved to Site-19 along with roughly a dozen others. She has since said, more than once, that she expected the transfer to be a step backward. It was not, though not for any reason she could have predicted.

Site-19's standing containment assignment for SCP-6714, as she was redesignated upon Foundation intake, fell, by administrative accident rather than design, under the oversight rotation of MTF Epsilon-11, the unit informally and, everyone involved agrees, extremely unfortunately nicknamed "Nine-Tailed Fox" decades before anyone at Site-19 had ever heard of Kiriko. Lieutenant Marcus Kade, then thirty-four, drew the rotation. Site-19 personnel present that day describe his first words to her, through the observation glass, as "so which one of us is the joke," which Kiriko has confirmed she found funny enough to actually laugh out loud, for what researchers on duty noted may have been the first time on record.

What followed was not, contrary to what the phrase "developed a relationship" might suggest to an outside reader, anything resembling special treatment. Kade visited during his own off-duty hours, logged every visit per protocol, and answered the questions Kiriko asked with the same directness he would have used with a new recruit. She wanted to know, first, why the doors worked the way they did. Then why some doors had two locks and some had four. Then, eventually and more pointedly, what would happen, procedurally, if a containment cell like hers ever failed during an active breach elsewhere in the facility, whether she would be protected, evacuated, or simply left. It was this last question, raised after a minor and unrelated breach three cells down in August of 2026, that Kade brought to his superiors and to Site-19's research staff directly, requesting formal authorization to answer it honestly rather than deflect it. Authorization was granted, on the condition the sessions be logged and reviewed. Neither side ever seemed to mind the paperwork.

By November, four months into an arrangement that had by then quietly stopped being purely procedural on either side, Site-19 experienced what internal Foundation communications still refer to only as the November Cascade. A single compromised containment cell, three sublevels below Kiriko's own, failed catastrophically at 02:14 on the 3rd. The cell held SCP-953, an entity of Korean origin publicly filed as a nine-tailed kumiho and privately regarded by every kitsune the Foundation has ever debriefed, Kiriko included, as something closer to a natural predator than a peer. Kumiho and kitsune are not merely different species by Foundation taxonomy. They are, in the specific words Kiriko herself used during her post-incident debrief, old enemies who have simply never stopped being at war, and SCP-953's escape triggered five further sympathetic containment failures across Site-19's lower levels within the hour, each response drawing further security resources away from the rest of the facility until Site command declared a Level 5 cascade and authorized whatever measures were necessary to prevent a total loss event.

Kiriko's own cell failed at 02:51, a secondary casualty of power redistribution rather than any action of her own. What she has stated, and what MTF Epsilon-11's after-action report independently corroborates from three separate accounts, is that she did not attempt to flee the facility. She went looking for Kade.

She found him instead already fighting SCP-953 alongside two other members of Epsilon-11, in a service corridor neither of them had any tactical reason to be defending except that it led, eventually, to Kiriko's own containment wing. The account every surviving witness agrees on is this: SCP-953, on sighting Kiriko, disengaged from the human combatants entirely and went for her instead, and what began as a firefight became, within seconds, something closer to what the Epsilon-11 after-action report calls, without any apparent irony, "a duel." Two nine-tailed entities, one silver-and-green and one the report describes only as "wrong to look at directly for more than a few seconds," met in the open for what was likely the first time either had encountered its own kind, hostile or otherwise, in centuries. It was not a clean fight. Kade was struck attempting to pull Kiriko clear of an opening strike she did not see coming, a wound to the abdomen that would prove, though no one recognized it in the moment, to already be fatal.

The battle that followed lasted, per surviving footage, six minutes. SCP-953 broke off and fled deeper into the facility once it became clear it would not win outright, a retreat Kiriko has described with open, uncomplicated fury as the one part of that night she has not made peace with. What she noticed only once the corridor went quiet was that Kade was gone.

He had not gone far. Facility logs place him at the manual override terminal for Site-19's Level 5 scuttle protocol, a fail-deadly nuclear contingency automatically initiated forty minutes into any uncontained Level 5 cascade and designed expressly to require a human hand at that specific terminal to abort it, so that no automated system could ever be tricked or compromised into stopping it early. Kade had removed his vest, sidearm, and rifle before making the walk, later found stacked neatly on the corridor floor precisely where Kiriko would pass them, and had sealed the blast door behind him manually rather than let it lock on its own, specifically, investigators concluded, so that Kiriko could not follow him through before it closed. She tried anyway. She did not reach it in time.

What the terminal logs show is a manual abort sequence entered in full, correctly, on the first attempt, at 03:34, six minutes before the automatic detonation window would have closed permanently and forty minutes after Kade had already taken a wound Site-19 Medical would later determine no field intervention could have saved him from. He did not radio for help. He did not wait to be found. Kiriko reached the terminal room eleven minutes later, having spent those eleven minutes doing exactly what she had once idly asked him to explain to her: working, unit by unit, room by room, with the two other members of Epsilon-11 still standing, helping re-secure or reassess three separate compromised containment cells along the way, because it was, by then, simply the next necessary thing to do, and she was, by then, someone who knew how. She found Kade seated against the terminal console, already gone, his hand still resting on the panel he had used to save the facility that had, six months earlier, been holding her prisoner in it.

There is no clean way to file what happened afterward, so this document will not attempt one. Kiriko was held for questioning for eleven days, a length of time Internal Security has since acknowledged, in writing, as longer than the circumstances warranted. Site-19's containment posture toward her was formally tightened and then, within the month, formally reversed entirely, once it became clear to every relevant authority, up to and including members of the O5 Council briefed directly on the incident, that the Foundation had, by pure administrative accident, ended up in possession of something considerably more valuable than a contained anomaly: a being of extraordinary age, extraordinary patience, and now-demonstrated loyalty, who had just spent a night proving, at cost, that she would rather help than run. The Intelligence Agency's formal request for her transfer was approved on 2027-01-09. She accepted it on the one condition she asked for, granted without argument: that she never be asked to work anywhere else.

She still keeps Kade's rifle. She has never asked for one of her own.

« END TRANSCRIPT »

---

## TITLE VI. Extra Information

**Weaponry & Equipment:** One HK416D carbine, serial number retained under Lt. M. Kade's original service record at Kiriko's request. One modified E-11 tactical vest, likewise retained under Kade's designation. Standard Foundation-issue sidearm, held but rarely carried.

**Emergency Anomalous Protocols:** In the event Kiriko is compromised, incapacitated by hostile anomalous influence, or otherwise required to be subdued, standard containment teams are advised that her primary anomalous properties (illusory concealment and minor elemental manifestation) are visual and thermal in nature and do not affect physical restraint. Silver-plated restraints are recommended per longstanding, if only partially substantiated, kitsune folklore, and have shown modest additional effect in controlled testing. Lethal force remains authorized only as an absolute last resort and requires sign-off from two of the following: Site-19 Command, MTF Epsilon-11 Command, or the Intelligence Agency Directorate. No single authority may authorize it alone.

**Anomalous Description:** Kiriko possesses four documented anomalous properties, consistent with standard kitsune physiology and not the product of any known external augmentation beyond the pigmentation damage described in Title II. (1) Selective visual concealment of ears and tails, sustainable indefinitely at rest and for approximately six hours under active use before requiring recovery. (2) Minor pyrokinetic manifestation ("kitsune-bi"), producing cool, non-igniting spectral flame usable for signaling, minor illumination, or short-range deterrence; it has never been observed to cause structural damage or serious burns in any recorded incident. (3) Sensory acuity significantly exceeding baseline human norms across smell and low-light vision. (4) Natural biological longevity correlated with tail count, currently nine, the theoretical maximum recorded in any Foundation-verified case. This is not immortality. Kiriko has stated plainly, when asked, that she does not believe herself unable to die, only unlikely to have died yet.

**Paratechnology:** None issued.

**Addendum:** Site-19's Chapel of the Fallen lists Lieutenant Marcus Kade among its dead. Beneath his name, in a hand that is not Foundation standard-issue and that no one on staff has taken any action to remove, someone has added seven words: he left the door open anyway.

---

## NOTICE

Effective immediately following the conclusion of all proceedings related to the events described above, Kiriko is to be transferred to another site, destination withheld pending final approval, to eliminate any perceived or actual bias arising from her continued proximity to Site-19 and to the surviving members of MTF Epsilon-11. This is a personnel and operational-integrity decision made jointly by the Intelligence Agency Directorate and Internal Security, and is not, and should not be construed as, any judgment on Kiriko's conduct.

Kiriko has not contested the order. She has requested, and been granted, standing authorization to return to Site-19's Chapel of the Fallen once annually, escorted, on the anniversary of the November Cascade. This is the only exception made to the transfer.

Title II of this record will be updated to reflect Kiriko's new Home Site upon completion of the transfer.
